relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: A soul may draw near with flour, oil, and frankincense.
A handful rises as memorial fragrance before YHWH.
The remainder becomes holy provision for the priests.
Pan-prepared flour and oil can draw near.
The offering is brought to priest and altar.
The memorial portion rises as soothing scent.
Leaven and honey are held back from this altar fire.
Firstfruits may be brought, though not as altar-smoke.
Every grain offering carries salt of covenant.
Fresh roasted grain becomes firstfruit offering.
Oil and frankincense crown the grain.
The memorial portion rises from grain, oil, and fragrance.

contemplative_note: Leviticus 2 says nearness can smell like bread, oil, salt, and frankincense. The holy includes daily food, covenant durability, and provision for service.
